rulings.Case Title: ALLISON JOHNSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Allison Johnson, By And Through Her Guardian Ad Litem'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Deft County Of San Mateo To Six Form Interrogatories And All Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR TUESDAY, APRIL 23. 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 3, Plaintiff Allison Johnson, By And Through Her Guardian Ad Litem'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant County Of San Mateo To Six Form Interrogatories And All Requests For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Mr. David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. The parties should be prepared to address on the hearing the central issue of the privacy rights of both the non-party adults and children who resided at the residence in San Mateo County and whose names were redacted in the documents produced by the County. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. David McDonald

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR SHOYKHET et al VS. TODD OPPENHEIMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Medical Examinations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 23, 2013, Line 20, Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Medical Examinations Denied. Defendants have not demonstrated good cause to re-open discovery to take the mental examination of Plaintiff. (CCP § 2024.050(b).) Defendants have not shown diligence in proceeding with discovery. Defendants were aware that Plaintiff's mental condition was at issue months prior to the filing of this action and that the medical records reviewed by Dr. Ruff were made available many months before he was first engaged as an expert by the defense. Defendants' delay in commencing review of the medical records made available by the Plaintiffs is inconsistent with the level of diligence which would warrant re-opening discovery. (See, e.g., Johnson v. Alameda County Medical Center (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 521 (when the moving party delays commencing discovery, the trial court did not abuse discretion by refusing to re-open discovery).) Further, prejudice will result to Plaintiff. The unavailability of a timely report from Dr. Ruff prior to trial will unfairly prejudice Plaintiffs' ability to analyze and rebut its findings. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. This motion appears duplicative of Defendants' motion to compel further responses to Defendants' interrogatories. Both parties are directed to bring into Court at the time of the hearing: (1) copies of any supplemental responses which were served after the filing of the motion and (2) a chart listing the responses that are still at issue. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. David McDonald

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ICHO VS. BALDWIN BOK YUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 23, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT BALDWIN YUNG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The motion is to vacate default is granted pursuant to CCP §473. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ICHO VS. BALDWIN BOK YUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 23, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT WORLDWIDE CAPITAL ASIA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The court construes this motion to set aside default as a motion under CCP 473. The motion is to vacate default is granted under the court's equitable powers. CCP §473.5 is not applicable to the instant facts. Defendant Worldwide Capital Asia Corporation has presented evidence which demonstrates that service was improper. Plaintiff's request to strike declarations in support of Worldwide Capital Asia Corporation and Baldwin Yung's motions to set aside default is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
